name: How to study smart and not hard?
slide1_center: How to study smart and not hard?
slide2_center:
Step 1. Choose what you want to learn.
Step 2. Teach it simply. Explaining it in your own words like you are explaining it to a child.
Step 3. Find gaps: spots that you don't understand. 
Step 4. Review and simplify: Relearn and explain more clearly.
Step 5. Repeat. Go over it again. To get a full understanding.
slide3_center: That translates and transcribes every lecture and zoom meeting into perfect notes, So that you don't waste your time Taking notes and use it to learn better.
slide4_center:
1. After learning: finish your study session.
2. REcall three things: Write down three key points that you remember.
3. RECall 2 details: Add specific facts or..Examples. 
4. RECall one question: write one question that you still have.
5. Review Later: To deepen your understanding.
slide5_center:
1. Set a task: The one you want to work on.
2. Work for 25 minutes: Fully for one POMODORO (i.e 25 minutes)
3. Take a short break: rest for 5 minutes.
4. Repeat: Use 4 pomodoro then.
5. A long break: for 15 to 30 minutes and then start again.

 from C to A.
slide2_center: When I have my important exam, I sleep with my notes because this helps me remember them. I also go to bed at 8:30 PM and wake up at 5:30 AM. This is scientifically a peak performance time for work.

slide5_center: I use a specific scent (Fruity or vanilla) while studying and then I use the same scent when I am in my exam hall. This scientifically tricks your mind to remember your notes.
slide6_center: I read 1 paragraph of a chapter as much as I can. I turn it into questions and do it for the entire chapter. Works when you are answering questions in real time.

slide2_center:
Simply rereading notes and textbooks creates an illusion of learning but doesn't improve recall. 
Use active recall: Close your book and try to recall key concepts from your memory without checking your notes.
slide3_top: 2. Studying for long hours without break.
slide3_center:
Studying for long hours without a break leads to mental fatigue and decreases productivity. 
Use pomodoro technique: Study for 25 to 50 minutes and then take a break of 5-10 minutes to give your brain some rest.
slide4_center: AI apps like Minutewise can turn your lectures and meetings into simple notes and give you time to actually understand and learn the concept.
slide5_center:

Past papers, quizzes and practice questions help identify weak spots and improve exam confidence.

name: Secrets about studying for different subjects that nobody will tell you

slide1_bottom: LAST ONE IS THE MOST IMPORTANT
slide2_center:
Stop rewatching the same tutorial. 
Do one hard question and force yourself to explain it out loud.
Struggle = learning.
slide3_center:
Don't just read the book.
Write the key ideas in your own words. While explaining it to a 10 year old. 
If you can't, you don't understand it.
slide4_center:
Memorization is a trap. Understand the ‘why’ behind the concepts. 
Pretend you are teaching a clueless friend.
slide5_center: Time timelines over notes. You don't see the connections, you'll never remember them. Use arrows, draw, make it visual.
slide6_center:
Maps > textbooks
Label Everything. The more you interact with the info, the more it sticks.

The ultimate tip is to stop making notes and use Minutewise AI to do it for you. 
More time = more learning.
